title: CPI(M) faults govt.’s claims on reduction in power tariffs
content: It is misleading the public by highlighting marginal reductions in electricity charges after imposing larger financial burdens through previous revisions, says party State secretary Srinivasa Rao

title: Rahul Gandhi should apologise for Emergency: J.P. Nadda
content: He made the statement in Patna during the Samvidhan Hatya Diwas (Constitution Murder Day) programme organised by Bihar unit of the BJP, on the 51st anniversary of the ‘Emergency’ declared by   Indira Gandhi in 1975

title: Four held in Ujjain for blowing up car in Muharram stunt; police rule out communal angle
content: Viral videos show a white van hanging through a crane about 40 feet from the ground which is blown up with firecrackers kept inside it, creating a significant explosion

title: Turned city of joy into city of death: West Bengal CM blames Trinamool regime for warehouse tragedy
content: With Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari blaming former Mayor Firhad Hakim who has aligned with the rebel group of Trinamool MLAs, Mamata Banerjee loyalists have sought the arrest of former Mayor.

title: FIR alleges assault on J&K police personnel by 40 Army men, including officers
content: “Army personnel climbed over the main gate and boundary wall and forcibly entered the police station premises,” say police; Army to extend “full cooperation” into the probe

title: Money laundering case: Actor Jacqueline Fernandez withdraws plea from Supreme Court
content: A Delhi court on May 30 ordered framing of charges against the actor, alleged conman Sukesh Chandrashekar and 15 others in the case

title: Health Ministry expands QR code-based drug traceability to vaccines, antimicrobials, and cancer medicines
content: Amended Drugs Rules bring vaccines, anti-cancer drugs, antimicrobials and NDPS medicines under Schedule H2; phased implementation to begin from July 2027

title: Passport application fee to go up from July 1
content: Aapplying for a fresh or reissued 36-page passport will cost ₹2,500, while a tatkal application will cost ₹5,000; rate for regular 60-page passports hiked to ₹3,500 and tatkal application will cost ₹6,000

title: In blockaded part of Manipur, an LPG cylinder now sells for ₹5,000
content: 40 people killed in Manipur since President’s Rule was revoked in February; Kangpokpi MLA and Deputy CM blocked from travel to Imphal; spike in prices of rice, petrol, no LPG cylinder supply in district

title: Digital Arrest scams: CBI conducts searches at over 80 locations across India
content: The arrested accused were involved in the incorporation of shell companies and the opening and operation of mule bank accounts. These accounts were reportedly used for laundering about ₹2 crore of suspected proceeds of crime.

title: Three States have arrived at ‘historic consensus’, says D.K. Shivakumar as new gates of Tungabhadra dam are inaugurated
content: Shivakumar said Karnataka, Andhra Pradesh and Telangana discussed key concerns raised by farmers, including the proposed Navali balancing reservoir, and the need to remove accumulated silt from the reservoir to improve water conservation and storage

title: Elephant chasers of Chalakudy are on constant vigil to avoid a ‘conflict’
content: In the Athirappilly forest area, teams of forest officials are constantly on the vigil, chasing away wild elephants that venture close to human habitations. An attempt to see firsthand how they operate in batches round the clock to avert human-wild elephant conflicts

title: Some Congress leaders signal possible rethink on Bidadi Township Project if farmers are unwilling to part with lands
content: The cautious position adopted by Karnataka Pradesh Congress Committee (KPCC) president B.K. Hariprasad and Home Minister Priyank Kharge on the proposed acquisition of agricultural land for the Bidadi Township Project, coupled with suggestions that the project may be shelved if farmers refuse to part with their holdings, is expected to strengthen the resolve of protesting farmers and provide fresh ammunition to the BJP-JD(S) alliance

title: Smart traffic system set to be launched by August, says official
content: Chennai will get 50 red-light violation detection cameras, 10 speed-violation systems and 58 traffic incident detection systems as a part of this.

title: Recruitment drive set to kickstart in Karnataka with number of jobs enhanced to 72,186
content: Public recruitment in Karnataka had been stalled since 2023 as a series of litigation over the reservation matters hit the processs

title: Another Bengaluru metro Purple Line snag at Cubbon Park Metro Station causes delays
content: According to the Bangalore Metro Rail Corporation Limited, the disruption occurred due to a problem with the train's door-closing mechanism. The issue led to a temporary halt in operations, affecting at least five subsequent train services and causing delays of nearly 20 minutes

title: Gold price crash explained: Why are gold rates falling and when will yellow metal recover?
content: In January 2026, gold prices hit $5595 - a life-time high - they are now trading at below $4,000. The prices are down 7.6% year-to-date. On the MCX, the decline has been lesser - at around 22% - largely due to a hike in import duties.

title: Playing Sooryavanshi at expense of a run-scorer would be 'unfair', says India coach
content: Speaking on the eve of the match, Kotak praised the teenager's ability but said team selection must also be fair to players who have already been scoring runs.

title: Mission Punjab 2027: AAP under pressure, Congress adrift; can BJP spring a Bengal-style surprise?
content: As Punjab gears up for the 2027 elections, a complex and competitive scenario unfolds. The AAP government grapples with controversies stemming from a sacrilege video and growing defections. In contrast, Congress is mired in its own turmoil, leaving room for the BJP to assert itself through robust campaigning efforts. The Shiromani Akali Dal’s fight for relevance adds another layer to a politically charged atmosphere, making the upcoming elections unpredictable.

title: Days after Meta CTO flagged low morale, company reverses two employee policies
content: Meta has reversed its controversial policy forcing engineers into an AI task force, allowing employees personal choice after widespread backlash. This move follows recent mass layoffs and an AI reorganisation that sparked unrest. The company also acknowledged past mistakes in its aggressive AI push, with CTO Andrew Bosworth admitting morale is at historic lows.  Concerns over an AI tracking tool have also led to its pause.

title: ‘She didn’t want the wedding, he wouldn’t back out’: Ketan Agarwal murder probe takes new turn
content: A shocking murder plot has unfolded in Pune, where a fiancée and her boyfriend are accused of killing her fiancé. Ketan Agrawal, a real-estate director, died after being pushed into a gorge at Lohagad Fort. Initially ruled an accident, police now allege his fiancée, Siya Goyal, and her boyfriend, Chetan Chaudhary, orchestrated the murder. Investigations suggest Goyal masterminded the plan, with Chaudhary allegedly assisting in the fatal push.

title: Japan's second richest man SoftBank’s Masayoshi Son junks his plan to retire in sixties
content: SoftBank's Masayoshi Son, 68, plans to lead the tech giant for another decade, focusing intensely on artificial intelligence and robotics. Dismissing 'AI bubble' fears as 'blasphemy,' Son believes AI's potential is just unfolding. He aims to achieve 'artificial superintelligence' and is backing this vision with significant investments in companies like Arm and OpenAI, alongside robotics acquisitions.

title: A van, a crane and a blast: Ujjain viral video shows dangerous mid-air stunt during Muharram procession
content: A shocking Muharram procession stunt in Ujjain saw a van hoisted 40 feet by a crane and detonated with firecrackers before thousands. While no one was injured, police have booked four individuals for endangering public safety. The dangerous display, which went viral online, has sparked serious concerns among locals regarding the potential for mass casualties from such reckless acts.

title: GM now replaces more than 1,000 workers with 50 robots at its flagship plant in the US
content: General Motors has introduced 50 collaborative robots at its Michigan Factory Zero, reportedly leading to over 1,000 job eliminations. This move, aimed at cost-cutting and efficiency, sparks criticism from the United Auto Workers union, which views it as a threat to jobs. GM claims the robots support human workers, but the UAW president calls it a "fight for humanity" amid broader automation concerns in the industry.

title: Shafali Verma joins elite club, matches Mandhana & Mithali in T20 WC records
content: Shafali Verma's explosive 53 off 34 balls against Bangladesh secured her third Player of the Match award in the ICC Women's T20 World Cup, placing her among India's elite. She also surpassed 500 career runs with an exceptional strike rate of 133.07, the highest in tournament history for batters with over 500 runs. India comfortably chased down Bangladesh's total, showcasing strong batting performances.

title: CM Punk recalls terrifying WWE Brazil incident involving armed confrontation
content: CM Punk recounted a terrifying 2012 WWE incident in Brazil where Chris Jericho's desecration of the national flag led to authorities drawing guns on them. The situation escalated rapidly, with law enforcement demanding an apology and attempting to confiscate Jericho's passport. Punk highlighted Brazil's strict laws regarding flag disrespect, contrasting it with other nations. Meanwhile, WWE reportedly plans CM Punk's return for SummerSlam, potentially on SmackDown.

title: Taylor Swift’s reported text to Blake Lively sparks fresh speculation
content: Taylor Swift and actress Blake Lively seem to have experienced a lot of setbacks in their bond as rumors swirl about how the pop star has cut her off ahead of her wedding to Travis Kelce which is set to happen next week. Even though both Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce have maintained their fair share of distance from Blake Lively, the pop star’s texts to her have raised eyebrows.

title: Who is Nicolas Pépé’s fiancée? Inside Teanna Trump’s life and career
content: Villarreal winger Nicolas Pépé is reportedly planning to marry Teanna Trump, a former adult film star. The couple, who have been public with their relationship since late 2024, are rumored to be heading towards an engagement. Their high-profile romance has garnered significant attention online and within football circles.

title: 'None of our MPs will split': Sharad Pawar's bold assertion in season of defections
content: Sharad Pawar confidently asserts his Nationalist Congress Party (SP) will remain united, dismissing claims of MPs defecting to Ajit Pawar's faction. His daughter, Supriya Sule, challenged the accuser to name the alleged defectors. This comes amid fears of poaching, following similar splits in Shiv Sena (UBT) and Trinamool Congress, where rebels exploited the Anti-defection Act's loopholes. Pawar's party, with 8 MPs and 10 MLAs, faces vulnerability.

title: Inside the Emergency: The ‘mess’ that triggered India’s darkest hours
content: Around fifty years ago, students at a government engineering college in Ahmedabad walked out over a steep hike in their monthly food bill. The cause was parochial; the consequences were not. From an OPEC meeting in Vienna in October 1973 to the midnight broadcast on All India Radio on June 25, 1975, a chain of economic shock, popular fury, political mobilisation, and institutional crisis unfolded without interruption. This report reconstructs that chain from primary sources — and asks, which links are held and which ones are more complicated than they appear.

title: T20 WC: Shafali Verma fires India past Bangladesh to keep semifinal hopes alive
content: India kept their Women's T20 World Cup semifinal hopes alive with a commanding five-wicket victory over Bangladesh. Shafali Verma's explosive 53 off 34 balls set the tone for a comfortable chase, despite persistent fielding concerns. Radha Yadav and Shree Charani impressed with the ball, restricting Bangladesh to 136/8. The win sets up a crucial final group match against Australia.

title: Horoscope today: Daily astrological predictions for June 26, 2026
content: Horoscope Today: Read daily horoscope predictions for June 26, 2026. Know all about the astrological events and influences that will be affecting each of the 12 zodiac signs. Our astrologer has analyzed the movements of the planets and the alignment of the stars to bring you the most accurate and up-to-date horoscope predictions for the day ahead. Get ready to discover what the stars have in store for you today.

title: Mamdani revolution: Are Democrats finally getting their own MAGA moment?
content: In a pivotal turn for New York City's politics, endorsed candidates of Zohran Mamdani have triumphed in the Democratic congressional primaries. This win signifies a reshaping of the political scene, highlighting a burgeoning progressive momentum that confronts the traditional party machinery. The implications on future elections and the broader national agenda of the Democratic Party are now sparking significant discussions, drawing parallels to the MAGA shift within the Republican Party.

title: FIFA World Cup: Qatar's Assim Madibo banned for five games over leg-break tackle
content: FIFA has issued a five-match suspension to Qatar's Assim Madibo following a dangerous tackle that caused a severe injury to Canada's Ismael Kone in a World Cup match. This shocking incident overshadowed Canada's landmark victory, and while the ban is harsh, it is lighter than the infamous nine-match suspension given to Luis Suárez in the past. Canada's challenge grows as they prepare to compete without their important midfielder.

title: Ram Temple donation case: After 8 booked in FIR, Akhilesh says 'big fish' being protected
content: Samajwadi Party chief Akhilesh Yadav has accused the Uttar Pradesh government of shielding "big fish" in the alleged Ram Temple donation embezzlement case. An FIR has been registered against eight individuals, but Yadav claims evidence might be tampered with under the guise of an SIT probe, suggesting a pre-determined outcome. This follows allegations of misappropriated funds, prompting a state-formed SIT and a new Supreme Court petition for a CBI-led investigation.

title: First in 96 years! Latham-Conway script history with record stand as NZ punish ENG
content: Tom Latham and Devon Conway etched their names in New Zealand's Test history with a colossal 317-run opening partnership against England at Trent Bridge. This monumental stand, the second-highest for New Zealand in away Tests, saw Latham score 151 and Conway 157, leaving England's bowlers searching for answers on a placid pitch. The partnership shattered previous records, setting a new benchmark in the series.

title: Shree Charani scripts history, becomes India’s most successful bowler in a T20 WC
content: Shree Charani etched her name in history, becoming India's leading wicket-taker in a single T20 World Cup edition with 12 wickets. Her stellar performance against Bangladesh, where she claimed two crucial wickets, propelled her past Poonam Yadav's record. Charani's exceptional bowling, coupled with Radha Yadav's three-wicket haul, restricted Bangladesh to 136/8, setting India up for a potential victory.

title: Lost money to cyberfraud? 2 things to do immediately and 1 mistake to avoid
content: Prime Minister Modi has urged states to expedite e-Zero FIRs for cyber fraud victims, aiming to streamline complaint registration and bypass jurisdictional hurdles. This push comes amid a surge in organized cybercrime, highlighted by a recent CBI crackdown. The article emphasizes the critical first hour after a fraud, advising immediate calls to the 1930 helpline and online reporting to maximize fund recovery chances.

title: Ram Temple donation row: UP Police files FIR 2 days after SIT gives report to CM Yogi; 8 booked
content: The FIR was lodged by the Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust member Krishna Mohan at the Ram Janmabhoomi police station under Sections 306 (theft by clerk or servant), 316 (criminal breach of trust), 317 (cheating) and 61 (criminal conspiracy) of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS), police sources said. According to the complaint, the accused allegedly embezzled donations offered by devotees at the Ram Temple and misappropriated trust funds through a criminal conspiracy.

title: From Amazon, ABB to Google, AirTrunk & more: Global giants commit billions of dollars to India
content: Large US technology companies have collectively committed billions of dollars to India, highlighting the country's growing importance as a key destination for cloud computing, artificial intelligence and deep-tech investments.

title: NBA trade rumor: Lakers eye Cavaliers center to support Luka Doncic
content: The Los Angeles Lakers would reportedly pursue a sign-and-trade sending LeBron James to the Cleveland Cavaliers for center Jarrett Allen, ESPN's Brian Windhorst said June 24, 2026 on ESPN Cleveland radio. James, an unrestricted free agent carrying a roughly $59.5 million cap hold, made $52.6 million last season. The proposal has gained traction because Allen would address the Lakers' long-standing need at center while complementing Luka Doncic and newly re-signed Austin Reaves.

title: India vs Ireland: The room they built for Vaibhav Sooryavanshi
content: A fifteen-year-old prodigy, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, has earned a private dressing room in Belfast, highlighting his extraordinary talent.  Despite his IPL heroics, selectors face a dilemma: debut him and disrupt a winning team, or wait for a less challenging opportunity. His mere presence, however, signals the arrival of the next generation of Indian cricket stars, a phenomenon already here.

title: In a first, Canada admits Khalistanis planted bomb on Air India 'Kanishka' Flight 182
content: Canadian prime minister Mark Carney on Tuesday bombing the "deadliest attack in our country’s history", saying that Canada stands against violent extremism in all its forms. "Forty-one years ago today, the bombing of Air India Flight 182 claimed 329 innocent lives, including 268 Canadians. It remains the deadliest terrorist attack in Canada’s history," the prime minister said in a statement.

title: BCCI Ombudsman disqualifies KSCA secretary after completing maximum term
content: Justice Arun Mishra (rtd), the ombudsman, in an order dated June 24, stated that Menon's tenure ended on Dec 16, 2025, nine days after he assumed charge following the elections held on December 7.

title: Video: How Siya Goyal, 'lover' Chetan plotted Ketan Agarwal's killing over coffee
content: A chilling plot unfolded as a fiancée and her lover meticulously planned a real estate director's murder. CCTV footage revealed their cafe meeting, where they finalized pushing him from Lohagad Fort. Despite multiple failed attempts and a staged accident, the couple was apprehended. Their extensive phone calls and a history of sabotage exposed the calculated conspiracy, leaving families devastated and prompting political intervention.

title: OFFSIDE: Too many goals at World Cup 2026? Wait for the knockout rounds…
content: Who is responsible for so many goals in this World Cup? The ball? Bad defending? Hydration breaks? Read this and more in today's OFFSIDE.

title: 'Tried to do as much research as we can on him': Tucker stunned by Sooryavanshi hype
content: Ireland captain Lorcan Tucker expressed awe at 15-year-old Indian batting prodigy Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's rapid rise, calling it "pretty incredible" for the modern era. Tucker highlighted the excitement Sooryavanshi generates, especially among Ireland's Indian community, and acknowledged his potential as a global role model.  Despite facing a tough series against a formidable Indian side, Tucker believes early wickets will be crucial for Ireland's chances.

title: Watch: Passport, Aadhaar, voter ID — what proves your citizenship? | Above the Fold | 25.06.2026
content: In this episode, we examine the debate over whether a passport proves citizenship and what documents actually establish citizenship rights. We also cover the deadly earthquakes in Venezuela, NCERT’s new Class IX chapter on the Emergency and the political reactions it has sparked, and more.

title: Madras HC refuses to interfere with ex-Minister Geetha Jeevan’s acquittal from disproportionate assets case
content: Justice G.K. Ilanthiraiyan dismisses a petition filed by a third party to condone a delay of 839 days in preferring a revision against the acquittal

title: M.P. High Court closes defamation case against Rahul Gandhi
content: Decision comes a day after the Congress leader expressed regret over his statement allegedly against Union Agriculture Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan’s son Kartikeya Singh Chouhan

title: Mahesh Dixit appointed Intelligence Bureau chief
content: He is among the Home Ministry officials who are engaged in talks with civil society leaders of Ladakh.

title: 125 Autism Support Centres mark A.P. government’s push for inclusive education, says MLA
content: Speaking at the valedictory session of two-day roundtable, Sundarapu Vijay Sreekumar calls for coordinated efforts among departments to ensure quality education for every child with special needs

title: Centre agrees to consider Kerala’s request to strengthen transport servcies
content: Transport Minister C.P. John meets Union Minister for Road Transport and Highways Nitin Gadkari in New Delhi on Thursday to discuss transport-related issues

title: Russia eyes stronger ties with Kerala across key sectors
content: India occupies a special place in Russia’s foreign policy and that the strategic partnership between the two countries will complete 80 years in 2027, says Russian Ambassador to India

title: MSMEs key driver behind country’s economic development, says NTR Collector
content: Addressing in a press conference, Lakshmisha says MSMEs play a vital role in the growth of a country’s Gross Domestic Product as they absorb local talent, promote self-employment and industrial development

title: King Charles Will Not Live At Buckingham Palace After Renovations
content: King Charles will not live at Buckingham Palace after its 10-year refurbishment finishes next year, royal officials said on Thursday, ending nearly two centuries of the central London landmark serving as the British monarch's primary residence.

title: Hezbollah Says Israel Killed 3 Civilians In Lebanon Amid US-Iran Talks
content: Iran-backed Hezbollah on Thursday accused Israel of a "flagrant" ceasefire breach, saying it launched a drone and killed three civilians in southern Lebanon.

title: Ahmedabad Police Foil Attempt To Exchange Banned Currency Worth Rs 39 Lakh
content: The Ramol police in Ahmedabad successfully intercepted a major illegal transit of discontinued currency, arresting two individuals who were carrying a massive cache of demonetized 500-rupee notes with a face value of 39 lakh rupees.

title: Man Kills Wife In Delhi After Dispute Over His Extramarital Affair: Cops
content: A 30-year-old woman was allegedly shot dead by her husband in south Delhi's Sangam Vihar area, with the accused fleeing the spot after the incident, police said on Friday.

title: If "Kali" Is Caught: After Suvendu Adhikari Remark, Ex-Mayor's Aide Arrested
content: "We don't know what has happened inside the Kolkata Municipal Corporation. If Kali is caught, all information will come out," Suvendu Adhikari said hours after the Kolkata warehouse collapse

title: Enforcement Directorate Questions Pinarayi Vijayan's Daughter
content: ED sources said that Veena has been asked to appear again as and when required, as part of the interrogation.

title: Bodies Of 4 Indians Killed In Qatar Explosion Repatriated: Officials
content: Bodies of four out of 12 Indian nationals who were killed in an explosion at a factory in the Ras Laffan Industrial City in Qatar are being repatriated to the country on Thursday, officials said.

title: India's Digital Revolution Enhancing Ease Of Living: PM To Dutch Queen
content: Modi expressed his delight in meeting the queen, who is also the UN Secretary General's Special Advocate for Financial Health and a long-standing global voice for financial inclusion.

title: Man Drowns 6-Month Pregnant Wife In Bucket After Fight In Faridabad
content: The accused allegedly assaulted the woman and later killed her by submerging her head in a bucket filled with water before fleeing the scene, police said.

title: AIADMK Rebel Factions Patch Up, EPS Restores Senior Leaders To Core Team
content: Following the recent assembly elections and an intense internal party feud, these senior leaders had previously been stripped of their organisational positions after breaking ranks with the high command.

title: Work Hour Extra For Development: Telangana Chief Minister To Government Staff
content: Telangana Chief Minister A Revanth Reddy said that the hard work of the employees will help in the successful implementation of every welfare scheme.

title: Weather Highlights: Relief From Severe Heat In Delhi-NCR This Week
content: Weather Today, Rain Alert Highlights: The Meteorological Department has not issued any weather warnings for the coming days.

title: On Camera, CPI(M), BJP Councillors Clash At Thiruvananthapuram Corporation
content: Mayor VV Rajesh, Deputy Mayor G Ashanath, and others were injured in violence at the Thiruvananthapuram Corporation.

title: Video: Iran President Left In The Sun? Shehbaz Sharif's 'Umbrella Moment' Goes Viral
content: It appears to show Pakistan Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif being protected from the sun as Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian and Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi walked beside him without an umbrella.

title: India Major Driver Of Global Growth Despite Iran War, External Headwinds: IMF
content: The IMF said it continues to project India's economy to grow by 6.5 per cent in fiscal year 2026-27, maintaining the upward revision made in April.

title: Air Force Helps In Rescue After Arunachal Flash Flood, Op To Resume Today
content: Despite extensive efforts conducted throughout the day, the missing persons could not be traced, State Disaster Management Secretary Dani Sulu said in a statement.

title: Paris Bans Alcohol Consumption, Sales Amid Heatwave
content: Paris police told reporters that alcohol consumption in the streets and other public places as well as takeaway alcohol sales will be banned from Friday amid intense heatwave.

title: Taking Measured Approach On Anthropic AI Models, In Talks With India: US
content: As both nations deepen their technological partnership, the focus has shifted toward balancing national security imperatives with the need for reliable, long-term access to models like Anthropic's Claude.

title: Speeding SUV Rams Car In Gurugram, Triggers Jam On Delhi Expressway
content: The impact damaged the Endeavour's front section. The damaged car stopped in the middle of the road, disrupting traffic on the busy expressway.

title: Anti-Terror Agency Arrests Congress Leader In Malda Judge Harassment Case
content: Chowdhury, a former Congress candidate from the Mothabari Assembly constituency, was taken into custody after prolonged questioning at the agency's Kolkata office.

title: Gulmarg’s gondola ride resumes after a month
content:
SRINAGAR: J&K CM Omar Abdullah Thursday re-opened Gulmarg’s popular gondola (cable car) ride that was closed on May 25 after a technical snag left more than 300 tourists stranded for nearly seven hours. Gulmarg’s gondola is among the world’s highest ropeways. Omar said operations were resumed after the manufacturer, a French engineering firm, visited the site and expressed satisfaction about the repair work. “We feared that Gulmarg would wear a deserted look once the gondola was shut but tourists continued to visit, and tourism activities remained largely unaffected. With the resumption of gondola services we expect the number of tourists will increase,” the CM said.

End of Article

title: Commanding Officer, Major among 30 named in FIR over ‘assault, rioting’ by Army at J&K police station
content: the commanding officer of 17 Rashtriya Rifles and a Major were among 30-odd Army personnel named in an FIR . they accused them of storming a police station, assaulting policemen on duty and damaging govt property . the group was armed with sticks, iron rods and service weapons, the complaint states .

content: apple's sharp increase in MacBook and iPad prices in india may be an early sign of broader price increases across the PC industry . the company raised prices across much of its Mac and iPad portfolio, citing an unprecedented surge in component costs . several MacBook Air and iPad models have also seen significant increases .
